info.title: AcquisitionControlAS API
info.description: The Acquisition Control Service (AcquisitionControlAS) allows samples to be submitted to an Acquisition System.

get.summary: Get a list of queued jobs.
get.description:
Returns a list of the jobs in the queue, both Running and Queued.  
Also returns job-specific details (including Priority and AcquisitionAppVersion).

400.description:
The client request is not valid

Possible error codes: 

 * **Validation** - The request is not valid.

401.description:
The request lacks valid authentication credentials

Possible error codes: 

 * **UnauthorizedAccess** - The request lacks valid authentication credentials.

403.description:
The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.The user might not have read access to the resource

Possible error codes: 

 * **NoAccess** - The user does not have access to the folder the sample list is stored into.

404.description:
The specified resource was not found

Possible error codes: 

 * **NotFound** - The resource was not found.

500.description:
An unexpected error has occurred

Possible error codes: 

 * **InternalServerError** - Internal Server Error.

post.summary: Submit sample to be acquired; allows numeric format (besides alphanumeric and vial) for sample position values
post.description:
Important: does not validate the sample position value against the tray dimensions.
The submitted sample is to be enqueued on an instrument system.  
The details (priority, location, etc) are submitted as part of the submissionRequestModel that accompanies each sample submission.  
Notes:  
AppCorrelationId - An identifier that was provided by a processing application (used in the post processing stage of the acquisition).  
JobCorrelationId - A unique identifier that may be used to correlate a sample list session with a job. 
The minimum accepted value of the Runtime property in the rows section of the SampleTable is 00:00:06. A lower value can cause the system to become unusable.

400.description:
The input data is invalid

Possible error codes: 

 * **Validation** - The request is not valid.

401.description:
The user is not authorized

Possible error codes: 

 * **UnauthorizedAccess** - The request lacks valid authentication credentials.

403.description:
The user is forbidden

Possible error codes: 

 * **NoAccess** - The user does not have access to the folder the sample list is stored into.

409.description:
The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource.

Possible error codes: 

 * **InvalidAuditLogReason** - The AuditLog reason is not in accordance with the corresponding folder Audit Trail Policy

 * **SampleSetMoved** - Sample set has been moved.

 * **InvalidSampleSetStatus** - The sample set is in an invalid state.

 * **SampleSetAcquiredWithDifferentInstrument** - Sample set has been acquired with a different instrument system.

500.description:
An unexpected error has occurred

Possible error codes: 

 * **InternalServerError** - Internal Server Error.

502.description:
Dependent service error

Possible error codes: 

 * **DependentServiceUnreachable** - The request could not be completed as the dependent service (PlatformAS, LND etc) is unreachable.

 * **DependentServiceNotFound** - The request could not be completed as the dependent service (PlatformAS, LND etc) cannot be found.

504.description:
Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

Possible error codes: 

 * **DependentServiceTimeout** - The request could not be completed as requests to the dependent service (PlatformAS, LND etc) timed out.

get.description: Returns job-specific details (including Priority and AcquisitionAppVersion).

post.summary: Send a pause job command to the system
post.description:
Only valid for a running Job.  
Pauses the execution of the job AFTER the completion of the running task (if any).  
Job status will be returned as 'Paused' when the status is requested.  
Note 1 - There is no timeout limit for a paused job; it will remain paused indefinitely.

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.The user might not have read access to the resource

404.description: The specified resource was not found

504.description: Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

post.summary: Send a resume job command to the system
post.description:
Only valid for a paused job.  
Resumes the execution of a paused job.  

Note - If the queue was stopped after the job was paused, and the queue resumed, the paused job will also resume running.

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.The user might not have read access to the resource

404.description: The specified resource was not found

504.description: Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

post.summary: Send an abort command to the system for a job that is in the 'Queued' state
post.description:
This will send a DELETE request to the LND for the specified job ID, to remove it from the queue.  
If the job transitions to running before the request is passed to the LND, then the job will be aborted and removed, and the next
job in the queue will automatically transition to a running state.

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.The user might not have read access to the resource

404.description: The specified resource was not found

504.description: Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

post.summary: Send an abort command to the system for a job that is in the 'Running' state.
post.description:
This command will disable the queue prior to deleting the specified job from the queue.  
All jobs will be put into 'Queued' state, and the system state will be 'idle'.  
The queue will need to be resumed before any further acquisitions can continue.

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.The user might not have access to the resource

404.description: The specified system or job could not be found based on the given parameters

504.description: Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

post.summary: Send a resume queue command to the system
post.description:
The system state will be set to 'Busy'.  
The first queued job in the queue will start running.  
Note - Any jobs that were paused prior to stopping the queue, will also be resumed when the the resume queue command is sent.

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed. The user might not have read access to the resource

404.description: The specified resource was not found

409.description: The specified system cannot perform resume due to current system state. eg Queue has already been enabled

504.description: Dependent services is running but is non-responsive.

post.summary: Send a stop queue command to the system
post.description:
The current task is stopped immediately and the job is put back on the queue.
The user must have "Acquisition > Interrupt Acquisition" permission.
An audit log will be created with an audit reason(depending upon the global audit trail reason policy "Acquisition > Interrupt Acquisition").

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials

403.description: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed. The user might not have read access to the resource

404.description: The specified resource was not found

409.description: The queue is already stopped, or system is in an invalid state

502.description: One or more of the dependent services is unreachable, unresponsive or responds with an error.  This may be caused by a network outage, firewall rules blocking access, the server may be offline, the service may have crashed, or there is a problem with the request

504.description: One or more of the dependent services is running but is non-responsive. This may be caused by a service crash.

get.summary: Get a list of real time plots for the given instrument system
get.description:
1) if the request header contains key="Accept" Value="text/event-stream" - it will open up a stream
   which will report all plots and then any new plots thereafter.       
2) if the request header doesn't contain the above, this function will make a simple request/response - 
   which will return a snapshot of the number of current plots.

200.description: A list of plots is returned for the given instrument system

404.description: The instrument system was not found

get.summary: Get a real time data stream for the given plot for the given instrument system.
get.description: The response will be long running and new data will be written to the response body as it is acquired.

200.description: A data stream for the plot data for the given plotId is returned for the given instrument system

404.description: The specified resource was not found

get.description:
There are four system states:  
Idle - System is ready to accept / run jobs.  
Busy - System is currently acquiring / setting up. Samples can still be submitted in the busy state.  
Error - System is in an error state, and acquisition is not possible.
NotReady - System is housekeeping and not ready to run jobs

400.description: The client request is not valid

404.description: No system was found with the specified system id

post.summary: Send request to stop the mobile phase flow
post.description:
The 'stop flow' request can be sent regardless of system state.  
There will be no response from the instrument regarding the status of the flow.

202.description: The stop flow request has been accepted

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The client request lacks valid authentication credentials

404.description: No system was found with the specified system id

502.description: One or more of the dependent services is unreachable, unresponsive or responds with an error.  This may be caused by a network outage, firewall rules blocking access, the server may be offline, the service may have crashed, or there is a problem with the request.

504.description: One or more of the dependent services is running but is non-responsive. This may be caused by a service crash.

post.summary: Send command to Reset the instrument system
post.description:
The 'reset' request will only be accepted when the system is 'idle', or 'in error'.  
If 'reset' is sent during an acquisition, the request is not accepted.

202.description: The request was successfully received by instrument system

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The client request lacks valid authentication credentials

404.description: No system was found with the specified system id

502.description: One or more of the dependent services is unreachable, unresponsive or responds with an error.  This may be caused by a network outage, firewall rules blocking access, the server may be offline, the service may have crashed, or there is a problem with the request.

504.description: One or more of the dependent services is running but is non-responsive. This may be caused by a service crash.

post.summary: Send request to set the instrument system to initial conditions
post.description:
The 'InitialConditions' request will only be accepted when the system state is 'idle'.      
Passes information from the selected Method to the instrument hardware, and establishes the initial conditions 
defined in the method e.g.temperatures, pump flow rate, detectors ON/OFF.

202.description: The request was successfully received by instrument system

400.description: The client request is not valid

401.description: The client request lacks valid authentication credentials

404.description: No system was found with the specified system id

409.description: Conflict because client queue was busy

originatingSoftwareModule.description:
The name of the software module or application which submitted the audit entry. 
This should be a customer facing name.

originatingSoftwareModuleVersion.description: The version of the software module or application which submitted the audit entry.

browserName.description: The name of the browser. Should be filled only for audit logs submitted by applications that are running in the browser.

browserVersion.description: The version of the browser. Should be filled only for audit logs submitted by applications that are running in the browser.

reason.description:
User specified reason for change that triggered the entry. The supplied entry must follow Audit trail policy.
maximum: 512
reason.example: Paused the sample set to fix a problem.

details.description: Detailed information related to the entry.

detail.description: The detail message for an audit section.
detail.example: Copy of item 'itemName', ID: 'ItemId', Version: 1, Folder: Company

ErrorCode.description:
Error codes that map to handled error use-cases inside API.
Values 1 - 99 are reserved for .NET error types
while 100+ are for application defined errors

details.description: Additional details relevant to the error, used only to enhance debug information
details.example: User has no access to requested folder

ErrorDetails.description: Error model data transfer object containing detailed error information

errorToken.description: A Guid token to allow the identification of the error inside the API logs (Guid format: B4A2E7D90C5C4E18A466DCAD9625606E)

message.description: Consumer friendly message describing the error

errorDetails.description: Additional details relevant to the error, used only to enhance debug information

ErrorModel2.description: Error model object containing error information

methodId.description: The method to use for initial conditions.

currentLine.description: The current running line of the job

totalLines.description: The total number of lines within the job

status.description: Status value as a localized string

incompleteJobs.description: List of incomplete jobs on the queue

jobListDateTime.description: Timestamp when job queue state was captured

jobListTicks.description: Elapsed ticks when job queue state was captured

systemId.description: Gets or sets instrument system ID

JobPriority.description: Priority of the job in the queue

stateTicks.description: Elapsed ticks when state was captured

timestampTicks.description: The timestamp on the lnd when the status was queried

tasks.description: Ordered list of tasks comprising the job

customProperties.description: Holds additional, third-parties related non-standard fields in a dictionary

acqMethodId.description:
The acquisition method id for the sample.
If populated this will override the default method.
Please reference the Methods API to access the available methods.

injectionVolume.description:
The injection volume in µL.
Minimum volume is 0.

runtime.description:
The acquisition runtime in minutes.
Minimum runtime is 0.

replicates.description:
The replicate count.
Minimum count is 1.

procMethodId.description:
The processing method id for the sample.
Please reference the Methods API to access the available methods.

sampleType.description:
Describes the purpose of the data in the experiment the user is performing,
and how the data will be used by the processing method.
(e.g. Standard, Unknown, QC, Blank).

extendedProperties.description: Holds additional, Waters related non-standard fields in a dictionary

SampleRow.description: Represents a single row in the sample table.

defaultMethodId.description: Method Id used if sample row does not specify a method

SampleTable.description: The list of samples to be acquired.

definition.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
            <sampleHolder xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
                          xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
                          xmlns="urn:www.waters.com/Instrument">
              <id>367F956D2EBC42A4BC7B97524CC9CD87</id>
              <geometryId>44C20BC5DF7A4713933EF8F87A5527A5</geometryId>
              <geometries>
                <geometry xsi:type="SampleTrayGeometry" id="44C20BC5DF7A4713933EF8F87A5527A5" name="ANSI - 48 Vial 2 mL Holder">
                  <siteGroups>
                    <siteGroup xsi:type="RowColumnSiteGroup">
                      <siteGeometry xsi:type="WellGeometry">
                        <defaultDrawOffset>0</defaultDrawOffset>
                        <defaultDispenseOffset>0</defaultDispenseOffset>
                        <volume>0</volume>
                        <diameter>10</diameter>
                        <depth>32</depth>
                        <shape>Round</shape>
                        <bottom>Round</bottom>
                      </siteGeometry>
                      <referencePosition>
                        <x>-52</x>
                        <y>-34.7</y>
                        <z>0</z>
                      </referencePosition>
                      <rows>
                        <count>6</count>
                        <spacing>13.5</spacing>
                        <lineOffset>None</lineOffset>
                        <labelType>Alpha</labelType>
                      </rows>
                      <columns>
                        <count>8</count>
                        <spacing>14.5</spacing>
                        <lineOffset>None</lineOffset>
                        <labelType>Numeric</labelType>
                      </columns>
                      <flow>HorizontalDiscontinuous</flow>
                      <labelScheme>AlphaNumeric</labelScheme>
                      <origin>TopLeft</origin>
                    </siteGroup>
                  </siteGroups>
                  <xDimension>128</xDimension>
                  <yDimension>86</yDimension>
                  <zDimension>38.299999</zDimension>
                  <shape>Rectangle</shape>
                </geometry>
              </geometries>
            </sampleHolder>

SampleTrayInfo.description:
Sample tray info.
Information on the physical characteristics of a tray.

sampleSetId.description:
The Sample Set id being acquired to. If this resource does not currently exist the acquisition will create it.
If the Sample Set already exists it will be appended to (versioned).

instrumentSystemId.description: The instrument system to be used for the acquisition.

folderId.description: The folder id where the Sample Set should be created.

userId.description: The id of the user who submitted the job.

username.description: The user name that submitted the job.

sampleSetName.description: The name of the sample set.

sampleSetDescription.description: The description of the sample set.

appName.description:
The name of the application that will perform processing for the acquisition.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM.

appId.description:
The name of the application that will perform processing for the acquisition.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM.

acquisitionAppName.description:
The name of the application that initiated the acquisition workflow.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM or Sample Submission if the acquisition was started directly from Sample Sub.
            This value is written into the origin field of the Sample Set.

workflowTypeId.description: The workflow type that identifies the table schema pre-registered with sample list API.

SubmissionInfo2.description: Context information about the job request being submitted with Priority and AcquisitionAppVersion

sampleSetId.description:
The Sample Set id being acquired to. If this resource does not currently exist the acquisition will create it.
If the Sample Set already exists it will be appended to (versioned).

instrumentSystemId.description: The instrument system to be used for the acquisition.

folderId.description: The folder id where the Sample Set should be created.

userId.description: The id of the user who submitted the job.

username.description: The user name that submitted the job.

sampleSetName.description: The name of the sample set.

sampleSetDescription.description: The description of the sample set.

appName.description:
The name of the application that will perform processing for the acquisition.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM.

appId.description:
The name of the application that will perform processing for the acquisition.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM.

acquisitionAppName.description:
The name of the application that initiated the acquisition workflow.
            This may be an analytical application like Peptide MAM or Sample Submission if the acquisition was started directly from Sample Sub.
            This value is written into the origin field of the Sample Set.

workflowTypeId.description: The workflow type that identifies the table schema pre-registered with sample list API.

priority.description:
The priority of the job within the queue.\
            A normal priority job will always be added to the end of the queue.\
            A high priority job will be added after any high priority jobs in the queue but before any normal priority jobs.\
            Note: This does not affect a job that is already running.

SubmissionInfoResponse.description: Response model containing submission information about the job submitted with Priority and AcquisitionAppVersion

injectionDataId.description: The correlation id for the task (injection).

acqMethodId.description: The acquisition method id for the task.

acqMethodName.description: The acquisition method name for the task.

sampleType.description:
Describes the purpose of the data in the experiment the user is
performing, and how the data will be used by the processing method.

customProperties.description: Holds additional, third-parties related non-standard fields in a dictionary

extendedProperties.description: Holds additional, Waters related non-standard fields in a dictionary

methodId.description: The method id for the task.

duration.description: The runtime of the task after conditions have been reached and the injection (if applicable) has started

tag.description: User-provided additional information for a task

TaskStateResponse.description: Task model representing the state of individual tasks in a job (includes user-defined tags and state)
